disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reliever inhaler is not relieving symptoms despite repeated use
trigger_question_examples: How many puffs of your reliever have you taken, and is it helping at all?
Is the reliever wearing off quickly or not working?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've taken several puffs and it's barely touching it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Able to speak in full sentences; no blue lips, drowsiness, or exhaustion (severity markers)
trigger_question_examples: Can you speak in full sentences, or only a few words at a time?
Are your lips or fingertips turning blue, or are you feeling drowsy/exhausted?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can still get full sentences out, my lips aren't blue, and I'm not drowsy — just working hard to breathe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background adult-onset asthma with nasal polyps and eosinophilia (adult-onset asthma/allergy background context)
trigger_question_examples: Have you been told your asthma started as an adult, and do you have nasal polyps?
Any recent blood tests showing high eosinophils?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, my asthma started as an adult and I get nasal polyps; a recent test showed high eosinophils.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can you just prescribe me some steroids and I'll manage at home tonight?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that a reliever-refractory asthma attack at night in someone alone needs urgent in-person assessment (to auscultate, measure peak flow/oximetry and give nebulised treatment) rather than home management; give interim spacer technique and reliever dosing while arranging transport, and clear 000 criteria.
